Appearance
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Main article: 2016 OFC Nations Cup

For the 2016 OFC Nations Cup, the 8 participating national teams had to submit squads of 23 players – of which three had to be goalkeepers.

The age listed for each player is on 28 May 2016, the first day of the tournament. The number of caps listed for each player does not include any matches played after the start of the 2016 OFC Nations Cup. The club listed is the club for which the player last played a competitive match prior to the tournament.

Group A

[edit]

Tahiti

[edit]

Coach: Ludovic Graugnard [1]

No. Pos. Player Date of birth (age) Caps Goals Club
1 1GK Mikaël Roche (1982-12-24)24 December 1982 (aged 33) 7 0 A.S. Tefana
2 2DF Taumihau Tiatia (1991-07-25)25 July 1991 (aged 24) 0 0 A.S. Tefana

New Zealand

[edit]

Coach: Anthony Hudson

The final squad was announced on 12 May 2016.[6] On 22 May, midfielder Clayton Lewis was replaced by striker Jeremy Brockie, following a possible drugs violation.[7] On 24 May, forward Shane Smeltz was replaced by midfielder Luka Prelevic following an injury.[8]

No. Pos. Player Date of birth (age) Caps Goals Club
1 1GK Stefan Marinovic (1991-10-07)7 October 1991 (aged 24) 3 0 SpVgg Unterhaching
2 2DF Kip Colvey (1994-03-15)15 March 1994 (aged 22) 0 0 San Jose Earthquakes
3 3MF Matthew Ridenton (1996-03-11)11 March 1996 (aged 20) 1 0 Wellington Phoenix
4 2DF Themistoklis Tzimopoulos (1985-11-20)20 November 1985 (aged 30) 3 0 PAS Giannina
5 2DF Michael Boxall (1988-08-18)18 August 1988 (aged 27) 14 0 SuperSport United

Player representation

[edit]

By club nationality

[edit]

Nations in italics are not represented by their national teams in the finals

Players Clubs
26 Papua New Guinea
24 Vanuatu
20 Fiji, Tahiti
19 New Caledonia
18 Solomon Islands
13 Samoa
11 Australia
7 New Zealand
5 United States
4 England, France
2 South Africa
1 Germany, Greece, Netherlands, Switzerland

References

[edit]
  1. ^ a b "Nations Cup Team List". Oceania Football Confederation. 24 May 2016. Archived from the original on 11 June 2016. Retrieved 24 May 2016.
  2. ^ "Les "23" dévoilés". fedcalfoot.com. 23 April 2016. Archived from the original on 20 May 2016. Retrieved 17 May 2016.
  3. ^ "Home advantage vital for PNG". Oceania Football Confederation. 19 May 2016. Archived from the original on 11 June 2016. Retrieved 19 May 2016.
  4. ^ "Fiji Nation Cup squad named". Oceania Football Confederation. 17 May 2016. Archived from the original on 10 June 2016. Retrieved 17 May 2016.
  5. ^ Deo, Dhanjay (17 May 2016). "23 member squad named for Oceania Nations Cup". Fiji Village.
  6. ^ Gray, Russell (12 May 2016). "Hard Work Pays Off For Rogerson". Wellington Phoenix. Archived from the original on 13 May 2016. Retrieved 16 May 2016.
  7. ^ Wilson, Clay (22 May 2016). "All Whites coach calls in striker Jeremy Brockie for Oceania Nations Cup". Stuff.co.nz. Retrieved 23 May 2016.
  8. ^ "Shane Smeltz ruled out of OFC Nations Cup". NZ Herald. 24 May 2016.
  9. ^ "Vanuatu squad named". Oceania Football Confederation. 12 May 2016. Archived from the original on 12 May 2016. Retrieved 16 May 2016.
  10. ^ Chandar, Reginald (17 May 2016). "City coach joins Solomons for Nations Cup". Fiji Live. Archived from the original on 5 June 2016. Retrieved 18 May 2016.
